title: Mid-Height Dresser with 5 Drawers (Board and Batten)
summary: A plywood-panel dresser in the board-and-batten style — 2x2-and-1x2 side frames wrapping ripped 3/4" plywood panels, sized for two small drawers and three full-width ones.
description:
This dresser's side panels are the whole design idea: a 2x2-and-1x2 frame wraps a piece of 3/4" plywood ripped to width, giving a board-and-batten look without solid-wood panel glue-ups. Rip your own plywood sheet into the two 14"-wide side strips first, saving the remainder for the dresser top — getting both from one sheet is what the materials list is built around.

The front frame boards are the single most consequence-heavy part of the whole build: they sit directly between the drawer faces, so their placement accuracy determines whether the finished drawer fronts read as an even grid or a slightly-off patchwork. Measure twice before committing any of them.

The top is squared against the dresser frame rather than the other way around — set the top on a flat surface, place the frame on top of it flush to the back with even overhangs on front and sides, and use the top itself as the squaring reference. This is backwards from how most casework gets assembled, and it's backwards on purpose: a flat panel is a much more reliable square reference than a leg-and-rail frame under its own weight.

Drawer boxes install inset 13/16" inside the frame, and drawer faces are cut about 3/16" undersize and shimmed into an even gap before final attachment — small numbers, but they're what keeps five drawer fronts reading as one clean face.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood side panels and top.

materials.name: 1x8, 30 linear board feet
materials.note: Larger drawer sides, front, and back.

materials.name: Euro drawer slides, 16", bottom corner mount

cutList.note: These sit between drawer faces — placement accuracy matters most here.

steps.title: Build the side frames
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 trim to the top of the 2x2 legs, pocket holes facing up. A single pocket hole and glue per joint is plenty, since the plywood panel adds many more reinforcing pocket holes in the next step.

steps.title: Rip and attach the side panels
steps.body: Rip the 3/4" plywood along the grain into two 14"-wide strips, saving the remainder for the top. Cross-cut to height, drill 3/4" pocket holes along the top and side edges, and attach with glue inside the frame from the previous step.

steps.title: Attach the bottom trim
steps.body: Attach the remaining 1x2 to the bottom of each side frame with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the front frame boards
steps.body: Attach the front frame boards to the side assemblies, placing them accurately since they sit directly between the drawer faces. Use a single pocket hole and glue per joint; switch to a 1-1/2" pocket hole with a 2-1/2" pocket hole screw for the bottom 2x2-to-2x2 joints. Repeat to attach to the second side panel.

steps.title: Add the center divider
steps.body: Measure and mark the center divider location carefully, then attach with brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Cut the top from the remaining plywood and apply edge banding to the outer edges with an iron. Place the top on a flat, level surface, then place the dresser frame on top of it, flush to the back with even overhangs on front and sides — use the top to square up the frame, checking that the overhangs are even and the frame lines run parallel to the top's edges. Attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws through the top 1x2s.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Measure each opening to confirm it matches the diagrams. Build the drawer boxes with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, and attach the 1/4" plywood bottoms with 3/4" brad nails and glue. Install the drawer slides inset 13/16" inside the frame.
materials: Euro drawer slides, 16", bottom corner mount

steps.title: Fit the drawer faces and finish
steps.body: Cut each drawer face about 3/16" smaller than its opening, shim into place with an even gap on all sides, and attach with 1-1/4" brad nails, avoiding hardware locations. Remove the drawers and secure the faces from inside with 1-1/4" screws at the four outer corners. Install pulls, sand, and finish.

images.alt: Board and batten style mid-height dresser with five drawers
